findings.description: No direct SQL execution — all database operations delegated to run_pre_gate1/run_post_gate1 (which delegate to ShadowStateDB, archivist, SessionManager). No raw SQL in this module.

Gate 1 checkpoint data — SessionManager.save() stores gate1_session.selected_direction and gate1_session.rejected_directions (user investment direction preferences). No credentials, no API keys, no PII in the checkpoint payload. SessionManager uses atomic writes (tmp → rename, session.py:57-60) preventing corruption on crash. Data stored as plain JSON in data/sessions/ — local filesystem only, no network exposure.
No credential exposure — API key handling delegated to run_pre_gate1 (which calls init_gateway). Orchestration layer never touches the key directly.

CLI IO handlers (_cli_input_handler, _cli_status_handler) are simple stdin/print wrappers for Gate 1 interaction. No injection surface — input() builtin returns raw string, no command parsing or evaluation.
datetime.now(timezone.utc) used for session_id generation — timezone-aware, compliant with root CLAUDE.md §3.2.

File at 173 lines (was 516 before extraction) — now 27 lines UNDER the 200-line glue soft threshold. Extraction of pre_gate1.py and post_gate1.py resolved the prior size concern. Clean single-responsibility: coordinates pre_gate1 + optional Gate 1 + post_gate1.

notes: Post-extraction orchestration is a thin coordinator (173 lines, down from 516). Gate 1 checkpoint stores only investment direction labels (no credentials/PII) with atomic writes preventing corruption. All database, file, and credential operations delegated to separately audited modules (pre_gate1, post_gate1, SessionManager). No new security risks introduced by extraction — risk surface is smaller than before.
